title: machaeranthera shastensis var. montana
intro: Machaeranthera shastensis var. montana, also known as Mountain Tucker or Mountain Waxy Cord, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Machaeranthera shastensis plant, a type of daisy-like flowering plant native to the western United States. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Machaeranthera shastensis var. montana include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from fever and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tinctures, salves, and infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms.
